mnemonics: GAP: Generate (using algorithm), Apply (inverse CDF), Produce (Gaussian numbers) to remember the process of generating Gaussian random numbers from uniform ones.
keyPoints: The Box-Muller transform is a common method to generate pairs of independent standard normal variables from pairs of independent uniform variables.
The inverse transform method can be applied to the Gaussian CDF (using approximations since a closed-form inverse does not exist) to generate random numbers.
Generated numbers should be tested for goodness-of-fit (e.g., using Q-Q plots or statistical tests) to ensure they follow the desired Gaussian distribution.
commonMistakes: Assuming a simple linear transformation of a uniform random variable will produce a Gaussian distribution (it does not).
Forgetting that the standard algorithms like Box-Muller generate standard normal N(0,1) variates, which must then be scaled and shifted for a general N(μ, σ²) distribution.
